violations.text: Prep unit #3 located in the prep kitchen area was not in good repair. Unit was observed with fans that are no longer functioning. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. PIC stated, repair technician is coming this afternoon to repair unit and replace fans. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Raw animal foods (such as chicken wings) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as arugula and condiments) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Raw food items were moved during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Meat sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC, food item was prepared the previous evening and was observed at temperatures ranging from 45-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Food item was disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The dessert display case was observed held at an ambient temperature of 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Establishment is not to store TCS food items in unit until an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less is maintained. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Various food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Lobster meat was observed thawed in prep unit #2 with intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood items packaged in ROP packaging shall be removed from its original packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Lobster meat was observed at 41-42 degrees Fahrenheit and voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Prep unit #3 located in the prep kitchen area was not in good repair. Unit was observed with fans that are no longer functioning. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
